§ 01Onboarding Complete

Dear {{sellerName}},

You've completed the onboarding series. Here's a summary of everything covered this week:

Onboarding Complete
You're Ready to Go
D1Welcome & overview. The 4-gate structure, what to expect, your Forhemit contact.
D2Document request. 15 documents organized in 3 tiers. Tier 1 due in 10 business days.
D3Calendar links. Kickoff call, weekly check-ins, and gate review scheduling.
D4Team introduction. Your deal team: trustee, ERISA counsel, appraiser, lender.
D5Today — First check-in. Quick status check and next steps.

Going forward: You'll receive weekly status updates from Forhemit every {{statusDay}}. Monthly check-in calls are scheduled for {{checkinDay}}s at {{checkinTime}}. Gate reviews are scheduled as each gate approaches.
§ 05A Note on the Process

The 120-day timeline is structured but not rigid. Things will come up — documents will take longer than expected, schedules will conflict, questions will arise. That's normal.

Forhemit's job is to keep the process moving while you focus on running {{companyName}}. If something feels overwhelming or unclear, say so. We'll break it down, re-prioritize, or bring in extra help.

You've taken the hardest step — deciding to explore employee ownership. The rest is process, and we've done this before.

—Forhemit Transition Stewardship
Forhemit Stewardship Management Co.
California Public Benefit Corporation
Stewardship Lead
